problem_statement: Increasing microplastic contamination in daily food items poses significant health risks. Current detection methods are time-consuming, expensive, and require lab infrastructure.

market_size.SAM: ~$1.2B (Food & beverage, packaged water, seafood industry testing)
market_size.SOM: ~$100M (Early adopters in regulatory agencies & premium food manufacturers)

market_drivers: Food safety is a priority in high-impact industries
Regulatory compliance pressure requiring cost-effective and portable solutions
market_information.competition: Current methods are lab-based, time-consuming, and expensive; competitors not specified in detail
market_information.validation: Customer discovery through interviews with senior scientists and professors in biotechnology

prototype_steps: Enzymatic degradation and size-based separation using microfluidics (lab on a chip)
Detection of microplastics using SERS technology
Identification and quantification of microplastics using AI
technology: Microfluidic chip for enzymatic degradation and size-based separation
Artificial Intelligence for identification and quantification

innovation: Portable microplastic detector offering real-time detection with high accuracy and specificity, combining enzymatic degradation, microfluidics, SERS detection, and AI analysis, which is faster, less expensive, and does not require lab infrastructure compared to existing methods.

impact.health: Reduces health risks by enabling quick detection of microplastic contamination in food.
impact.regulatory: Helps food brands and regulatory agencies comply with stricter food safety regulations.
impact.market: Provides a desirable, simple, inexpensive, and accurate alternative to lab-based testing, benefiting brands, labs, and regulatory bodies.

revenue_model.subscription: ₹30,000 – ₹1,00,000 per year for cloud-based AI analytics and reports

cost_structure.fixed_costs: ₹53L – ₹103L (R&D, prototyping, chip fabrication, substrate development, certifications, manufacturing setup)
cost_structure.variable_costs_per_unit: ₹7,900 – ₹19,700 (microfluidic chip, reagents, SERS substrate, electronics, assembly)

go_to_market.product: Portable microplastic detector with size-based separation and SERS detection
go_to_market.place: Online platforms, retail stores, specialty stores, B2B sales to food manufacturers, regulatory agencies, research labs
go_to_market.price: ₹6,000 for consumers to ₹16,000 for industries and laboratories
go_to_market.promotion: Digital marketing, partnerships with nutritionists, health professionals, food brands and labs, content marketing, events

feasibility.team: Multidisciplinary team with expertise in Biotechnology, Electronics and Communication, AI and Machine Learning
risks: Market adoption dependent on willingness to pay for portable real-time detection
